title: From Chatbots to Agents
concept: Traditional LLMs are passive chat systems; AI agents are active systems that use the model as a core reasoning engine to plan and act.

exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of traditional LLM chatbots when trying to complete real-world tasks?

options.text: They are passive and only respond when prompted.

exercises.explain: Traditional chatbots are passive because they only respond when you type a prompt, rather than taking independent actions.

cards.title: Beyond the Chat Box
cards.body: We are all used to chatbots that wait for us to type a prompt before they reply. But what if AI could actively take action, use tools, and solve problems for you while you sleep? That is the leap from chatbots to AI agents!

cards.title: What is an AI Agent?
cards.body: An AI Agent is an active system that uses a Large Language Model (LLM) as its 'brain' or reasoning engine. Instead of just replying to text, it can write down a plan, use digital tools, and complete complex goals on its own.

cards.title: The Librarian vs. The Assistant
cards.body: Think of a traditional chatbot like a digital encyclopedia: it sits on the shelf waiting for you to open it and read a page. An AI agent is like a personal research assistant: you tell them what you want, and they stand up, search the library, take notes, and bring you the final report.

cards.title: Agents in Action
cards.body: If you ask a traditional chatbot to book a flight, it might give you tips on how to search. An AI agent, however, can look up flights, compare prices, choose the best option based on your calendar, and use a booking tool to reserve the ticket for you.

cards.title: More Than Just a Long Prompt
cards.body: Many people think agents are just chatbots with longer prompts. In reality, agents are entirely different systems. They have loops that let them observe the world, make decisions, use external tools, and correct their own mistakes.

cards.title: Passive vs. Active
cards.body: See how the user's role changes when switching from a basic chatbot to an active AI agent.

left.text: Passive. You must prompt it, copy its output, run it yourself, and tell it what to do next.

right.text: Active. You give it a goal. It plans, uses tools, checks its own work, and finishes the task.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes traditional LLM chatbots?

options.text: They actively search the web and fix their own coding mistakes.
They are passive systems that wait for a user prompt to generate text.
They can automatically control your computer mouse and keyboard.
They do not use language models at all.

exercises.explain: Traditional LLMs are passive; they only respond when prompted and do not take independent action or use external tools on their own.

exercises.prompt: True or False: AI agents use language models as reasoning engines to plan and make decisions.
exercises.explain: AI agents use the LLM as a core reasoning engine to make decisions and decide which tools to use.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how agents solve complex tasks.
exercises.sentence: Unlike passive chatbots, an AI agent can create a step-by-step plan to achieve a goal.
exercises.explain: Traditional chatbots just generate the next word in a conversation, whereas AI agents can create a multi-step plan to solve a complex goal.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is acting as an AI agent?

options.text: A coding assistant that writes, runs, tests, and fixes code until it works.
A translation app that changes English to French.

exercises.explain: The coding assistant that runs code and fixes bugs is behaving as an agent because it actively uses tools and iterates to solve a problem. The other two are passive search or text generation.

exercises.prompt: You want to build a system that automatically reads incoming emails, checks your calendar, and books meetings for you. What kind of system are you building?

options.text: A chatbot that uses nicer words to say it cannot help you.
A database that stores answers in alphabetical order.
An active system that uses an LLM to plan, decide, and use tools.
A system that only responds when you tell it exactly what to write.

exercises.explain: An AI agent uses the language model to think and make plans, but it also has the power to run those plans and use external tools to get things done without you guiding every single step.
